key_specs: {"piece_count":"2 x 12-piece puzzles","dimensions":"approx 21 x 30 cm each completed","materials":"Chunky wipe-clean cardboard","included_items":"Two farm-animal jigsaws; talk guide on box","other":"Strong colour/background clues on each piece"}
product_description_under_30_words: Two chunky 12-piece farm jigsaws in one box. Wipe-clean cardboard pieces with bold background colours so the picture stays visible while they work.
ember_verdict: This is the step up from peg posting: real interlocking pieces, but the pig and cow scenes stay readable while they turn and compare. Strong visual clues keep frustration low — a solid third-birthday gift if peg puzzles are already easy.
rank_rationale: #1 over #2: Farm Friends is the clearest 12-piece strategy step-up with the deepest Amazon proof; jungle theme at #2 is the same spec so farm wins as the default first buy.
why_it_fits: 12 pieces hits the strategy brief without jumping to 20+; UK-made Orchard quality; clear picture-first design; strong Amazon and brand-site proof.
caveats: Age mark says 2+ — honest for nearly three, not a fresh 18m buy. Start with one puzzle out; save the second for a win later in the week.
gift_note: Reliable small-box gift under £10; wrap-friendly and familiar on UK nursery shelves.
ownership_note: Skip if you already own Orchard 12-piece farm or jungle sets from a younger sibling — borrow a different theme first.
safety_notes: Small parts — supervise if mouthing is still common at the younger end of nearly three.
review_quality_note: Parents praise chunky pieces and wipe-clean board; 12 pieces ambitious before 30m — right band for 34–36m.
evidence_sources: Amazon UK B08R6BWGX1 | orchardtoys.com First Farm Friends
preloved_signal_note: Common on Vinted; check all 24 pieces present — Orchard sells replacement pieces.
substitute_if_unavailable: First Jungle Friends Jigsaw Puzzles (longlist rank 2)
evidence_notes: Mumsnet names Orchard progressive jigsaws among trusted toddler puzzle brands.
buying_factor_memo: Five factors set order for nearly-three first jigsaws. First, real interlocking strategy: 6–12 piece scenes with a visible picture beat peg boards and 2-piece warm-ups already mastered. Second, age overlap with 34–36m: Orchard/Ravensburger/Bigjigs lines marked 2+ or 18m+ pass FF age gates; honest 3+ only marks (Big Wheels, Galt 4-in-a-box) stay on the longlist. Third, progressive value: a box that steps 4→12 or mixes 2/3-piece bridges before 12-piece wins shelf space. Fourth, review depth on working UK primaries — Amazon and Orchard on-site where scrapeable. Fifth, distinct parent jobs: farm 12-piece plan, jungle theme swap, thick-card warm-up, wooden durability, and emergency-vehicle quick wins without five near-duplicate farm sets.

caveats: Near-duplicate of rank 1 — own one theme, not both, unless gifting to a different household.

product_description_under_30_words: Four farm mini-jigsaws in one box — two, three, four and five pieces. Extra-thick Ravensburger cardboard with bold animal pictures.
ember_verdict: Box says 18m+ but at nearly three you want the four- and five-piece puzzles, not the two-piece warm-ups. Thick pieces survive repeated turning — low-stakes practice before a 12-piece Orchard.
rank_rationale: #3 over #4: Ravensburger thick-card warm-ups build turn-compare-try with massive review proof before the wooden 6-piece trays; wood wins durability but fewer aggregated UK reviews.
why_it_fits: Excellent cardboard quality; huge Amazon review base; teaches strategy on 4–5 pieces before scaling up.
caveats: 18m box mark fails freshness if marketed as a new-stage buy — position as use the top two puzzles only. Two-piece puzzles are too easy for most 34–36m children.
gift_note: Cheap, recognisable brand — fine as a stocking filler if they have no jigsaws yet.
ownership_note: If peg puzzles and 2-piece sets are mastered, start on the 4–5 piece puzzles or skip to rank 1.
safety_notes: Standard small-parts guidance for youngest marked age.
review_quality_note: Long-running UK favourite; multiple parent blogs corroborate thick-piece quality.

product_description_under_30_words: Three wooden safari jigsaws, six pieces each, in separate trays. Chunky FSC wood pieces with bold scene pictures.
ember_verdict: For parents who want something that survives being stepped on. Six pieces needs a small plan, but the trays keep the picture visible. Good counterpoint to cardboard if you already own Orchard farm sets.
rank_rationale: #4 over #5: Wooden 6-piece interlock is a distinct material/format with honest 2+ mark; Rescue Squad is brilliant for quick wins but only 2–3 pieces — less strategy for confident nearly-threes.
why_it_fits: Real interlocking wood at 6 pieces; UK heritage brand; budget price; distinct from cardboard picks.
caveats: Smaller completed size than Orchard 12-piece — less floor presence. Fewer aggregated online reviews than Amazon bestsellers.
gift_note: Compact wooden set — nice for families avoiding plastic; under £6.
ownership_note: Also available as Big Movers (vehicles) if safari animals are duplicated at home.
safety_notes: Conforms to current European safety standards per manufacturer.

product_description_under_30_words: Six emergency-vehicle jigsaws in one box — two-piece and three-piece puzzles — plus six character play pieces for matching play.
ember_verdict: Brilliant confidence builder before 12-piece sets: turn a piece, compare the picture, try again — without a table full of parts. Vehicle-mad nearly-threes will line up drivers beside toy fire engines.
rank_rationale: #5 completes the set: Rescue Squad covers cautious starters and vehicle fans when 12-piece feels a stretch; it trails wood and Ravensburger warm-ups on strategy depth but wins on hook and Amazon proof.
why_it_fits: Real interlocking pieces at 2–3 count; huge Amazon review base; distinct emergency-vehicle hook.
caveats: May feel easy within weeks for confident puzzlers — move to rank 1 when they ask for more pieces. Bag small play pieces if mouthing persists.
gift_note: Compact box under £7; strong for children who love sirens and rescue vehicles.
ownership_note: Different enough from farm/jungle 12-piece sets to own alongside — unless you already have Orchard transport first-puzzles.
safety_notes: Small play pieces — bag for younger nearly-threes still mouthing.
review_quality_note: Consistent praise for first jigsaw and vehicle matching play.
